principle: One roof, no shortcut paths. Content Orchestrator decides what to make; Creative Engine owns visual composition; Social Engine owns static campaign planning/scheduling; Video Engine owns reel build/QA/Telegram approval; shared scripts/docs/assets/calendar live under the same roof.

creative.role: Shared visual/image composition engine for static posts, product cards, model/product frames, value cards, brand end cards, blog OG assets.

social.role: Campaign planner and static-post scheduler. Selects products, builds approval packs, validates campaign gates, and hands live posts to Meta scheduler after owner approval.
must_use: creative engine for all final visual assets

video.role: Reel job queue, voiceover, frame sequencing, QA, hosting, and Telegram approval gate.
must_use: creative engine for product hero/value/brand/model-holding frames
must_not_own: bespoke visual templates duplicated from Creative Engine

approval_gates.reels: video-engine build -> jobs/review -> reels_bot Telegram approve -> publish_approved.py --live
approval_gates.visual: real product cutout/source check before generated/model frame composition

scripts.role: Shared operational scripts and active social/publish utilities.

docs.role: Reference docs, runbooks, and Claude/OpenClaw/deploy notes.

generated_assets.role: Generated image assets, GMC repair candidates, prompts, and reusable output artifacts.

social_calendar.role: Historical and current social calendar working assets, references, backups, and review sets.

migration_policy.phase_1: Physical engine and shared-store directories live under Content Orchestrator.
migration_policy.phase_2: Active code, runbooks, generated scheduler paths, and local configs use Content Orchestrator paths directly.
migration_policy.phase_3: Root workspace shortcut symlinks and internal engine shortcut symlinks are removed.
migration_policy.phase_4: Any future engine/content folder must be created under Content Orchestrator first.
